name: popover
title: Popover
description: A small floating panel anchored to a trigger. Use for transient, non-modal supplementary content ‚Äî tooltips with rich markup, settings panels, lightweight forms ‚Äî that is dismissed by clicking outside or pressing Escape.

meta.docs:
# Popover

A small floating panel anchored to a trigger. Use for transient, non-modal supplementary content ‚Äî tooltips with rich markup, settings panels, lightweight forms ‚Äî that is dismissed by clicking outside or pressing Escape.

## When to use

- **Anchored auxiliary UI** ‚Äî compose `Popover` + `PopoverTrigger asChild` + `PopoverContent`. Use `defaultOpen` (uncontrolled) or `open` + `onOpenChange` (controlled).
- **Non-button trigger** ‚Äî wrap any element in `<PopoverTrigger asChild>`.
- **Placement around the trigger** ‚Äî pass `side` (`top`/`right`/`bottom`/`left`) and `align` (`start`/`center`/`end`) on `<PopoverContent>`.
- **Pixel-precise spacing** ‚Äî `<PopoverContent sideOffset={8} alignOffset={4}>`.
- **Content-hugging width** ‚Äî default content is `w-72`; override with `<PopoverContent className="w-auto">`.
- **Headed content** ‚Äî use `<PopoverHeader>` + `<PopoverTitle>` + `<PopoverDescription>` for structured layout.

## When not to use

- **Focus-trapping, blocking overlay** ‚Äî use `Dialog` (or `AlertDialog` for destructive confirmations).
- **Single-text hover hint** ‚Äî use `Tooltip`.
- **Action menu** ‚Äî use `DropdownMenu` (`MenuButton` / `OverflowMenu`).
- **Single-select picker** ‚Äî use `Select`.
- **Anchored to a text reference** ("hover for details") ‚Äî use `HoverCard`.

## Composition

- Content always renders into a portal at `<body>`. Ancestor `overflow:hidden` will not clip it.
- There is no `caret`/arrow primitive in the wrapper ‚Äî import `<PopoverPrimitive.Arrow />` from `@radix-ui/react-popover` and place it inside `<PopoverContent>` if needed.
- `dropShadow` is always on (`shadow-md`); strip with `className="shadow-none"`. There is no `highContrast` or `isTabTip` variant ‚Äî restyle via Tailwind.
meta.migrationGuide:
# Popover ‚Äî Carbon ‚Üí shadcn migration guide

## Structural differences

Carbon's `Popover` is a **wrapper component** that takes the trigger and content as ordered children: the first child is the trigger, the last child is `<PopoverContent>`. Open state is controlled via the `open` prop on the wrapper.

shadcn's `Popover` is a **Radix-based slot composition**: explicit `<PopoverTrigger>` (typically `asChild`) and `<PopoverContent>` (Portal'd). Open state is owned by Radix internally; supply `open`/`onOpenChange` for controlled mode or `defaultOpen` for uncontrolled.

| Concept | Carbon | shadcn |
|---|---|---|
| Wrapper | `<Popover open>{trigger}{content}</Popover>` | `<Popover><PopoverTrigger>{trigger}</PopoverTrigger><PopoverContent>{‚Ä¶}</PopoverContent></Popover>` |
| Trigger | first child (any element) | `<PopoverTrigger asChild>{element}</PopoverTrigger>` |
| Content | `<PopoverContent>` as last child | `<PopoverContent>` (rendered via Radix Portal) |
| Position | `align` prop (`top`, `top-start`, `top-end`, `right`, `right-start`, ‚Ä¶) | `side` prop (`top`/`right`/`bottom`/`left`) + `align` (`start`/`center`/`end`) |
| Open state | `open` prop on wrapper | `open` + `onOpenChange` (controlled) or `defaultOpen` (uncontrolled) |
| Caret / arrow | `caret` boolean (shows triangle) | not modeled ‚Äî render `<PopoverPrimitive.Arrow>` from Radix manually |
| High contrast | `highContrast` prop (dark popover) | none ‚Äî set `bg-foreground text-background` via `className` |
| Dropshadow | `dropShadow` prop (toggle) | always on (`shadow-md`); strip via `className="shadow-none"` |
| Auto-align | `autoAlign` (Carbon's collision detection) | always on via Radix's `Positioner` (no opt-out) |
| Tab tip variant | `isTabTip` (renders as a tab) | not modeled ‚Äî would require custom composition |

## Carbon features missing in shadcn

- **`caret` prop** ‚Äî Carbon renders a CSS-drawn caret/arrow pointing to the trigger. shadcn requires you to import `@radix-ui/react-popover`'s `Arrow` and place it inside `<PopoverContent>`.
- **`highContrast`** ‚Äî Carbon's "dark popover on light background" treatment. shadcn has no toggle; override classes (`bg-foreground text-background border-foreground`).
- **`isTabTip`** ‚Äî Carbon's tab-style popover variant (used for inline help, looks like a tab attached to the trigger). No shadcn primitive; would need a custom shape.
- **`dropShadow={false}`** ‚Äî Carbon offers a flat (no-shadow) variant. shadcn always carries `shadow-md`; remove with `className="shadow-none"`.
- **Compound `align` values** ‚Äî Carbon's `top-start`, `top-end`, `right-start`, etc. are a single token. shadcn splits into `side` + `align` (which is more flexible but verbose to translate).
- **`autoAlign={false}`** ‚Äî Carbon lets you disable collision-aware repositioning. shadcn's Radix backbone always repositions; opt out by setting `avoidCollisions={false}` on `<PopoverContent>` (Radix prop, accepted via spread).

## shadcn features missing in Carbon

- **`asChild` slot** ‚Äî `<PopoverTrigger asChild><RouterLink>‚Ä¶</RouterLink></PopoverTrigger>` lets you keep your trigger as any element without losing Radix's open/close wiring. Carbon expects raw children.
- **Explicit `Portal` rendering** ‚Äî shadcn's content always renders into a portal at the end of `<body>`. Avoids `overflow:hidden` parent clipping by default. Carbon's popover renders inline.
- **`onOpenAutoFocus` / `onCloseAutoFocus`** ‚Äî Radix exposes lifecycle hooks for focus management. Useful for forms; Carbon has none.
- **`onEscapeKeyDown` / `onPointerDownOutside`** ‚Äî fine-grained dismissal control (e.g., prevent closing on outside click for long forms). Carbon collapses these into a single `onClose`.
- **`align` granularity** ‚Äî `start` / `center` / `end` independent of `side`. Lets you align a `bottom` popover to either edge of the trigger.
- **`sideOffset` / `alignOffset`** ‚Äî pixel-perfect spacing. Carbon offers no offset prop.
- **Animation primitives** ‚Äî entry/exit animations driven by `data-state` attributes; tweak via Tailwind's `data-[state=open]:animate-in` etc. Carbon's CSS transitions are baked in.

## Behavioural differences

- **Open semantics** ‚Äî Carbon: fully controlled (`open` is required). shadcn: uncontrolled by default; pass `open` only if you need to drive it from React state.
- **Default content width** ‚Äî Carbon: hugs the content. shadcn: `w-72` (288px) by default. Override with `className="w-auto"` for content-hugging.
- **Dismissal** ‚Äî Both close on outside click and Escape. shadcn additionally respects `onPointerDownOutside` for pointer-vs-keyboard differentiation.
- **Focus trap** ‚Äî Both trap focus inside the open content for keyboard users (Radix) and Carbon's Floating-UI-based engine.

## Migration checklist

1. Wrap children: replace `<Popover open>{trigger}{content}</Popover>` with `<Popover><PopoverTrigger asChild>{trigger}</PopoverTrigger><PopoverContent>‚Ä¶</PopoverContent></Popover>`.
2. Move open-state from `open` on the wrapper to `open`/`onOpenChange` (controlled) or `defaultOpen` (uncontrolled).
3. Translate `align`: `top` ‚Üí `side="top" align="center"`, `top-start` ‚Üí `side="top" align="start"`, etc.
4. Replace `<PopoverContent>` Carbon child markers ‚Äî same name, but in shadcn it's the actual Radix content, not a marker. Check that any layout you put inside still makes sense without Carbon's default padding.
5. If you used `caret`: import `<PopoverPrimitive.Arrow>` from `@radix-ui/react-popover` and render it inside `<PopoverContent>` (style with `fill-popover`).
6. If you used `highContrast`: override classes ‚Äî `bg-foreground text-background border-foreground`.
7. If you used `isTabTip`: build a custom compound (a button with attached tab-shaped panel); no primitive exists.
8. If you used `dropShadow={false}`: add `className="shadow-none"`.
9. If trigger is not a real button (e.g., a `div`): wrap it in `<PopoverTrigger asChild>` so Radix can attach handlers.
